sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=40, col=33).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 3, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: After clicking the purple ring center (21, 39), the white diamond moved from its original position (left side) to the ring's location. A new trail now connects: white diamond (at ring) → purple square → dark gray diamond. The purple square also repositioned along this trail.
- Goal model: Move the white diamond along the trail to reach the dark gray diamond at the end.
- Action model: Clicking on the ring placed the white diamond there. Next, clicking on the purple square should advance the white diamond along the trail.
- Plan: Click on the purple square center to advance the white diamond toward the dark gray diamond.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 2 of about 22 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me analyze what changed after clicking on the purple square center (40, 33):
Before:
- White diamond was at the purple ring location (around row 21, col 39)
- Purple square was at rows 38-42, cols 31-35
After:
- The white diamond (node 17) is now at [[38, 33], [40, 35], [42, 33], [40, 31]] - this is at the purple square's previous location!
- The white diamond has a purple center (node 18 at [40, 33])
- A new purple square (node 21) is now at [[47, 29], [47, 31], [48, 32], [50, 32], [51, 31], [51, 29], [50, 28], [48, 28]] with magenta center at [49, 30]
- The dark gray diamond (node 27) is still at [[57, 27], [59, 29], [61, 27], [59, 25]]
- Light gray trail continues: nodes 19, 20, 25, 26
So the pattern is clear now:
1. Click on the purple ring → white diamond appears there
2. Click on the purple square → white diamond moves to the purple square's location, and a new purple square appears further along the trail
3. The trail extends toward the dark gray diamond
The white diamond is now at the purple square's location (around row 40, col 33). The next purple square is at rows 47-51, cols 28-32 with magenta center at (49, 30). The dark gray diamond is at the end.
I need to keep clicking on the purple square to advance the white diamond along the trail until it reaches the dark gray diamond.
Let me click on the new purple square's magenta center at (49, 30).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking on the purple square moved the white diamond to that square's location. A new purple square appeared further along the trail toward the dark gray diamond. Pattern: click the purple square to advance the white diamond along the trail.
Goal model: Advance the white diamond along the trail until it reaches the dark gray diamond at the end.
Action model: Each click on the purple square's center moves the white diamond to that position and spawns a new purple square further along the trail.
Plan: Click on the new purple square center (49, 30) to continue advancing.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the new purple square's magenta center at (49, 30)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 49, 'col': 30}])
print(f"Action result: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"\nKey objects after action:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'M', 'G'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
